steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage states that conspicuous editing “would induce a more critical stance toward a protagonist”—which is the opposite of the identification produced by invisible editing and POV shots.

steps.title: Match to the correct answer
steps.content: ”Lessening identification with the protagonist” directly describes what a critical stance achieves. The other choices either wrongly suggest viewers remain unaware of editing, introduce director identification that Mulvey doesn't propose, or conflate stylistic attention with emotional response. That matches choice B — B is correct.
